channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Bobby Witt Jr. and Cole Ragans lead the Royals over the Orioles 1-0 to open an AL Wild Card Series
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — As Bobby Witt Jr. prepared to make his much-anticipated playoff debut for the Kansas City Royals, he took a moment after the final notes of the national anthem rang out at Camden Yards on Tuesday to soak it all in. The bright lights on a cloudy afternoon.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mets continue wild ride with 8-4 win over Brewers in playoff opener
item.description: Mark Vientos hit a tiebreaking, two-run single during a five-run outburst in the fifth inning as the indefatigable New York Mets continued their thrilling week by beating the Milwaukee Brewers 8-4 in an NL Wild Card Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Travis Kelce loses bet to Patrick Mahomes – what are the terms?
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs stars Patrick Mahomes and Travis Kelce made a wager ahead of a game between Texas Tech and Cincinnati over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bills' Von Miller suspended by NFL for 4 games for violating personal conduct policy
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Buffalo Bills edge rusher Von Miller was suspended four games by the NFL on Tuesday for violating the league's personal conduct policy. The NFL did not reveal the reason for the suspension, which comes as Buffalo (3-1) prepares to play at the Houston Texans on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adams says no word from Pierce since Raiders coach appeared to like trade talk on social media
item.description: Davante Adams said on his weekly spot on the “Up and Adams Show” that he hadn’t heard from Las Vegas coach Antonio Pierce since a screenshot appeared of Pierce appearing to like a social media post about possibly trading the Raiders star wide receiver.
item.dc:creator: Mark Anderson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appreciation: Dikembe Mutombo, a Basketball Hall of Fame player, had impact far beyond the game
item.description: The finger wag. The enormous smile. The unmistakable voice. Dikembe Mutombo played defense at a level and with a flair that few others in basketball history ever possessed, all among the many reasons why he's immortalized in the Hall of Fame. On the court, he stopped people.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Rose speaks with KTLA 5 in final interview
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball's all-time leader in hits and one of baseball's most divisive figures, died Monday at his home in Las Vegas. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Pete Rose, legendary baseball player, dies at 83
item.description: Nicknamed "Charlie Hustle," the 17-time All Star holds the Major League Baseball record for the most career hits.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Bucs prep for Falcons in Primetime TNF after big win over Eagles
item.description: After beating the Philadelphia Eagles the Tampa Bay Buccaneers now lead the NFC South with a 3-1 record.
item.dc:creator: C.H., WFLA, NBC

item.title: Broncos def. Jets on the road 10-9
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Week 4 of the NFL season saw the Cincinnati Bengals claim their first victory of the year with a 34-24 win. Wide receiver Ja'Marr Chase showcased his speed and balance, sprinting 63 yards for a touchdown.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Fred Warner's pick-6 helps the 49ers snap a 2-game skid with a 30-13 win over the Patriots
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Even after all the success the San Francisco 49ers had the past few seasons, a two-game skid and a string of injuries early in the season can raise some doubts for any team. Considered those answered for one day at least.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Elly De La Cruz hits 2-run triple in 10th to lead Reds to 3-0 win over Cubs
item.description: Elly De La Cruz hit two-run triple in the 10th inning to break a scoreless tie and the Cincinnati Reds ended their season with a 3-0 shutout of the Chicago Cubs on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox beat playoff-bound Tigers 4-0, stay at 121 losses going into finale
item.description: Sean Burke pitched two-hit ball over five innings, Andrew Benintendi tied his career high with his 20th homer and the Chicago White Sox beat the playoff-bound Detroit Tigers 4-0 on Saturday to stay at 121 losses going into their season finale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reds bring 4-game losing streak into matchup against the Cubs
item.description: Cincinnati Reds (76-84, fourth in the NL Central) vs. Chicago Cubs (82-78, second in the NL Central) Chicago; Saturday, 2:20 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: It's official: This Los Angeles Angels team is the worst in franchise history
item.description: The Los Angeles Angels, in their 64th season in franchise history, made history on Thursday — just not the good kind.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: A man says Ohtani's 50-50 home run ball was ripped out of his hands. Now, he's suing
item.description: The world's best baseball player completed Major League Baseball's first-ever 50-50 season last week, and one fan will get rich from it.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks gets goals from new veteran additions but fall to Red Wings, 4-2, in preseason opener
item.description: CHICAGO — The Blackhawks took the United Center ice Wednesday night in game action for the first time this season, and a couple veteran additions to the roster — including a former first-round draft pick back with the team again — found the back of the net right away.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Former NBA MVP Derrick Rose announces retirement
item.description: Derrick Rose's last act as an NBA player came in the form of a letter to the game of basketball, addressing the highs and lows that he experienced over a 16-year pro career. And with that, his career ended on his terms. Rose, the No.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Derrick Rose, Chicago native and NBA MVP with the Bulls, announces retirement from pro basketball after 16 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Derrick Rose, a Chicago native and city basketball legend in every sense of the word, is retiring from professional basketball. Rose, who grew up in Englewood on the city's Southwest Side and was a prep star at Simeon before being drafted No.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Brett Favre says Parkinson's diagnosis came after struggles with his right arm
item.description: Brett Favre revealed Tuesday that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease while speaking to a congressional committee.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
